column1: flow
user -> area — type=association; label="user has an area"; explanation="a user can have multiple areas"
area -> categories — type=association; label="area has categories"; explanation="an area can have multiple categories"
categories -> reports — type=association; label="category has reports"; explanation="a category can have multiple reports"
user -> reports — type=association; label="user has reports"; explanation="a user can have multiple reports"
influencer -> user — type=association; label="influencer has user"; explanation="an influencer can have multiple users"
flow 1: user -> area -> categories -> reports -> user
flow 2: user -> reports
the system allows users to create and manage areas, categories, and reports.
users can view and manage their own reports.
the system has a login and registration feature.
the system has a home page and a category list page.
user -> login - type: association; label: "user logs in"; explanation: "user initiates login process"
login -> system - type: association; label: "login request"; explanation: "login request sent to system"
system -> login - type: association; label: "login response"; explanation: "system responds with login result"
login -> user - type: association; label: "login success"; explanation: "user is logged in successfully"
login -> user - type: association; label: "login failure"; explanation: "login fails"
flow 1: user -> login -> system -> login -> user - "user logs in, system processes, and returns login result"
flow 2: none
the diagram models the login process, where the user initiates the login, the system processes the request, and returns a login result.
the system checks the user's credentials and returns a success or failure message.
the user is either logged in successfully or shown an error message.
utente -> ui — type=association; label="inserisci dati registrazione"; explanation=the user inserts registration data
ui -> firebaseauthentication — type=association; label="creaaccount(email, password)"; explanation=create a new account with email and password
firebaseauthentication -> firebaserestore — type=association; label="validatetoken(token: string)"; explanation=validate a token
ui -> firebaseauthentication — type=association; label="validatetoken(token: string)"; explanation=validate a token
flow 1: utente -> ui -> firebaseauthentication -> firebaserestore -> utente -> ui -> firebaseauthentication -> firebaserestore -> utente -> ui -> firebaseauthentication -> firebaserestore
the user inserts registration data, creates a new account with email and password, validates the token, and then validates the token again.
the diagram models the registration process of a new user in a system.
the main flows involve the user, the user interface, the firebase authentication system, and the firebase restore system.
the system checks for errors and provides feedback to the user.
the user can create a new account with email and password and then validate the token.
utente -> ui — type=association; label="inserisci segnalazione"; explanation=insert signal
ui -> firebase/firestore — type=association; label="dati incompleti o errati"; explanation=incomplete or incorrect data
firebase/firestore -> firebase/storage — type=association; label="carica immagine"; explanation=upload image
firebase/storage -> firebase/firestore — type=association; label="conferma invio segnalazione con ente assegnato"; explanation=confirm signal with assigned entity
flow 1: utente -> inserisci segnalazione -> dati incompleti o errati -> carica immagine -> conferma invio segnalazione con ente assegnato
the diagram models a sequence of events where the user inserts a signal, which may be incomplete or incorrect, leading to a loop of re-trying the insertion.
the user's action is followed by the upload of an image, which is then confirmed with an assigned entity.
the system uses firebase/firestore and firebase/storage for data storage and management.
